title: Episode 23 Armisaels fusion with Unit 00 and Rei
display_title: Episode 23 Armisaels fusion with Unit 00 and Rei
wikitext:
[]: Episode 23' - Armisael's fusion with Unit 00 and Rei             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Sat Aug 14, 2004 10:29 pm http://www.evaotaku.com/director_cut/Episode23/index.html Well now that I have seen these images, Eva has raped my mind even after finishing the entire series, again. I think it's obvious that this scene foreshadows certain EoE scenes. But how was the angel doing that? I'm guessing since it had direct contact with Rei it could do that, but Rei hadn't been absorbed into Lilith, so does that mean any angel could have initiated Thrid Impact? And why was the angel doing that? To destroy Shinji by fooling him? I am so tired and confused and I have many more questions but one at a time.......I think I will claw at my face until the next post.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Aug 14, 2004 10:59 pm This Angel, Armisael, can be argued as an adaptive Angel. Other Angel's can be considered adaptive as well, or as being tailored to certain situations. In the case or Armisael, it fuses with Unit 00, and gestates (so to speak) and sprouts out of the Unit. This brown mass features bits of every Angel thus far. What it was becoming is questionable. Part of its original form becomes like Rei, and clings to Unit 00, echoing Rei's "desire to be one with Ikari". She reverses her AT field drawing the Angel into the Unit, and at that time, causing massive Bio-Fusion. Just as the Unit explodes, the Unit fully transforms into the spitting image of "Uber Rei" (Rei/Lilith from EOE). I don't think this is the Angel trying to cause Third Impact. I think its searching for a form to assume, and it does, but just as it does, it goes "Kablooie". I think that absorbing Rei would have had some disasterous consequences. Possessing the soul of Lilith, (if possible) would undoubtedly create a powerful Angel. Which leads me to a question of my own. If an Angel could absorb another Angel's soul, which soul would assume dominance over the vessel? Toy with that idea. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 22, 2004 8:14 am The Eva Monkey wrote: She reverses her AT field drawing the Angel into the Unit, and at that time, causing massive Bio-Fusion.

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Sun Aug 22, 2004 9:15 am Man, I've got to get these director cuts.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Sun Aug 22, 2004 2:00 pm kosure wrote: Man, I've got to get these director cuts.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Aug 22, 2004 2:15 pm Reichu wrote: In NGE, you only see souls being absorbed by entities that don't have them -- i.e., the Evas in the Contact Experiments. Why would an entity that already has a soul want to absorb one? Is this even possible in the NGE universe?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 22, 2004 6:30 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Yes, it is entirely possible, Unit 01 absorbed Shinji. It had already obtained the soul of Yui Ikari.

[]:             Quote Post by lesmiserables » Tue May 18, 2010 12:32 am I love this episode – especially the angel tower. After each angel was killed, their souls just hung around the earth with out a body. Armisael fused with 00 and used her body matter to ‘birth’ new bodies for the angel's souls to settle in. That’s what I’m thinking anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Tue May 18, 2010 9:42 am This brown mass features bits of every Angel thus far.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue May 18, 2010 9:58 am Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion wrote: I'm also curious as to how Zery was among his brethren with the Angel Tower because when Unit 01 ate his S2 Core shouldn't his soul have remained inside of it since it wasn't damaged/blew up?

[]:             Quote Post by lesmiserables » Tue May 18, 2010 10:14 am SaltyJoe wrote: Aslo, i personally think that the Angel Tower had more to do with genetics and biology, rather than soul hax.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Tue May 18, 2010 12:29 pm SaltyJoe wrote: S2 organ != core, at least not necessarily (and the show itself never clears this up).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 24, 2010 11:55 pm SaltyJoe wrote: Aslo, i personally think that the Angel Tower had more to do with genetics and biology, rather than soul hax.

[]:             Quote Post by lesmiserables » Tue May 25, 2010 12:34 am Reichu wrote: Did you come up with that on your own, or assimilate it from the Internets?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 25, 2010 3:27 am I've taken it to PM to spare the souls of the general public.
